confidence.description: DEPRECATED in v1.7/v1.3. Superseded by ambiguity_assessment. Retained for backward-compat parsing only.

rationale_for_precedent.description: Concise human-readable rationale for the verdict, focused on the decisive signal. Max 1200 chars.

applicable_precedent_ids.description: case_id strings of APPLICABLE precedents for bidirectional linking.

proposed_atom_tags.description: Skill's proposed atom tags across 7 dimensions (C/S/P/R/E/M/X).

simulated_claim_chain.description: Claim IDs from the skill's re-evaluation of the case against the proposed atoms.

engine_alignment.description: Comparison between skill verdict and engine simulation from the input payload.

watch_outs.description: Potential issues or edge cases the reviewer should be aware of.

ambiguity_assessment.description: Content Ambiguity assessment (Defensibility Pillar B). Evaluates how genuinely ambiguous the content is, absorbing the old confidence rubric signals and content-boundary analysis into a single structured object.

classification.description: L1 = low ambiguity (clear-cut), L2 = moderate (reasonable people could disagree), L3 = high (genuinely borderline, escalation-worthy)

flip_plausibility.description: How plausible is it that the content genuinely supports the alternate verdict? 0.0 = implausible, 1.0 = equally valid.

factors.description: 1-4 human-readable reasons for the ambiguity classification.

weakest_atoms.description: Atom keys with lowest tagging confidence.

precedent_support.description: Precedent backing strength. strong = >=2 APPLICABLE agree. partial = 1 APPLICABLE or split. none = 0 APPLICABLE.
